title: Cyperus beyrichii
taxonomy: Cyperus beyrichii is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. This classification places it within the larger monocot clade, alongside other plants such as grasses and lilies. The Cyperaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sedges and rushes. Cyperus beyrichii is further categorized within the genus Cyperus, which comprises over 700 species of sedges. The exact taxonomic classification of Cyperus beyrichii may vary depending on the source or most recent research.
uses: Cyperus beyrichii has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its use in treating conditions like arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
